threats.title: Weak Password Policy Leads to Credential Theft
threats.description: The system relies on a standard username/password authentication mechanism without strong password policies. Attackers can use brute-force attacks, dictionary attacks, or credential stuffing techniques to compromise user accounts.  Lack of multi-factor authentication (MFA) exacerbates this risk.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, password cracking, phishing campaigns targeting users with weak passwords.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (minimum length, complexity requirements, regular password changes).
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all user accounts.
Implement account lockout policies after multiple failed login attempts.

threats.title: Insufficient Audit Logging Hinders Accountability
threats.description: Basic logging and monitoring are implemented, but the level of detail and alerting thresholds are undefined. This makes it difficult to track user actions, identify suspicious activity, and establish accountability in case of security incidents or fraud.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious actors can intentionally alter or delete logs to cover their tracks, making it impossible to determine who performed a specific action.
mitigations: Implement comprehensive audit logging, capturing all relevant user actions and system events.
Establish clear alerting thresholds and configure monitoring systems to generate alerts for suspicious activity.
Regularly review audit logs to identify potential security incidents and investigate anomalies.

threats.title: Lack of Data Classification Leads to Data Leakage
threats.description: The absence of data classification information means the system doesn't know what data it’s protecting. This creates a significant risk of exposing sensitive data to unauthorized users or systems, especially if data is transmitted without proper encryption or access controls.
threats.affected_component: All System Components (Data Storage, Network Transmission)
threats.attack_vector: Unencrypted data transmission, misconfigured access controls, vulnerabilities in application code exposing sensitive data.
mitigations: Implement a data classification scheme to categorize data based on its sensitivity.
Enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit.
Implement strict access controls based on the data classification scheme.

threats.title: Insecure API Access Allows Privilege Escalation
threats.description: If the system utilizes APIs, and those APIs aren't properly secured, an attacker could exploit vulnerabilities to gain elevated privileges, potentially gaining full control of the system.
threats.attack_vector: Broken authentication, authorization flaws in API design, lack of input validation, insecure API key management.
mitigations: Implement robust authentication and authorization mechanisms for all API endpoints.
Enforce the principle of least privilege, granting users only the minimum necessary permissions.
Regularly audit API access logs to detect and prevent unauthorized access.

threats.title: Insecure Data Storage Allows Modification
threats.description: Without knowing the data classification or storage mechanisms, there's a risk that sensitive data is stored without proper protection against modification.  This could include vulnerabilities in the database or application code allowing unauthorized data changes.
threats.attack_vector: SQL injection, application vulnerabilities allowing direct data modification, compromised application code.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization to prevent SQL injection and other injection attacks.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ data loss prevention (DLP) mechanisms to monitor and prevent unauthorized data modifications.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class api_gateway,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Password Policy Leads to Credential Theft
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insufficient Audit Logging Hinders Accountability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-778
    owasp: A09:2021
    threat_category: Repudiation
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Data Classification Leads to Data Leakage
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insecure API Access Allows Privilege Escalation
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insecure Data Storage Allows Modification
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
